- - 1 book containing (the) Hymns of St Gregory (of Nazianze) on the - - - VIII x book(s) containing - - - x book(s) containing the (Gospels) of - - - and Mark x new book(s) containing the General Epistles x parchment book(s) containing (Writings) of holy fathers IX x book(s) containing - - - 4 books containing the (Gospels) of Luke and - - - x new book(s) containing the Epistles of Paul 1 parchment book containing the Epistles of Paul 1 new book containing the Acts x parchment book(s) containing (a selection) of the General Epistles x book(s) containing - - - X From the monastery (handed over) to the deacon, as follows: 1 small - - - altar 1 - - - chalice

title: Notification of a theft of hay
summary: This papyrus contains a notification from the nauarchus to the riparii of the nomos of a theft of hay (or grass still in the fields).
translation_text: P. L. Bat. 13 8 (English) In the year after the consulate of our lord Theodosius, eternal Augustus, for the ninth time and of Flavius Constantius the most illustrious patricius for the third time on the 24th of Pharmouthi. To the Flavii Joseph and Theodoulos, riparii of the Oxyrhynchite nome from Flavius Theodoros, son of Theon, nauarchus of the same town. I do not understand how it has been possible, while in the present situation peace reigns everywhere – for fear of the power of our most eminent chief magistrate – that some evil-doers have invaded my land and stolen the hay - - -

title: Sale of land
summary: This papyrus is a fragment of a sale of land, including seven witnesses.

title: Letter (description)
summary: Obscure; perhaps Christian precepts embedded in a letter

title: Christian feveramulet
summary: This papyrus is an amulet, which should protect the wearer against fever.
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19. 20] Le Christ ....... Le Christ est apparu. Le Christ a souffert. Le Christ est mort. Le Christ est ressuscité (des morts). Le Christ est enlevé (au ciel). Le Christ est roi. Le Christ sauve ... cus (?) qu'a mis au monde Gennaia, de toute fièvre brûtante et de toute sueur froide, quotidienne, journalière, déjà, déjà, vite, vite.

title: Military document
summary: The contents of this papyrus must remain obscure.

title: Military document
summary: The contents of this papyrus must remain obscure.

title: Act prescript
summary: This text is no doubt a contract, but its nature cannot be specified. The text begins with a full dating formula.

title: Mumientafel
summary: Name on a wooden tablet
translation_text: Tamoupis

title: Mumientafel
summary: A woman's name and age on a wooden tablet
translation_text: Senplenis, wife of Pata- -, - - 48 years old.

title: Wooden tag for the mummy of Tnaphersaïs
summary: A wooden tag for a mummy with order or permission for the burial
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19:] Petosiris, son of Oteyris, says to Totoês, son of Imuthês, The Overseer of the Mystery of Osorbuchis: "perform burial for Tnaphersaïs, daughter of Hôros, her mother is Nb-t-wḏꜣ". Written in year 15, fourth month of the pr.t-season, day 6 (6 Pharmuthi). [Translation from Short Texts 2, including corrections:] Petosire, son of Uojhor, is the one who says to Tutu, son of Imhotep, the overseer of the mystery of Osobuchis: 'Perform burial for Tnafershai, daughter of Hor, her mother Nebuoj.' Written in year 15, 4th month of the šmw season (Mesore), day 6.

title: Wooden tag for a mummy
summary: A wooden tag for a mummy with order or permission for the burial
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19. 37:] Year 20, firds month of the ꜣḫ.t-season, day 16 (= 16 Thoth). The scribe Teôs, son of Pamenôs the elder, the priest who enters, of the fourth phyle, says to Totoês, the Overseer of the Mystery of Osorbuchis: "perform burial for ..., son of Hatrês". Teôs, son of Pamenôs the elder, the priest who enters, of the temple of Ḥḳ, wrote (this). [Translation from Short Texts 2. 428 with correction:] Year 20, 1st month of the ꜣḫ.t-season (Thot), day 16. Scribe Jeho, son of Pamenh the elder, the priest who enters, of the 4th phyle, is the one who says to Tutu, the overseer of the mystery of Osorbuchis: 'Perform burial for Miumaht, son of Hatre.' In writing of Jeho, son of Pamenh the elder, the priest who enters, of the temple of Heka.

title: Wooden tag for the mummy of Hatrês
summary: A wooden tag for a mummy with an order or permission for the burial
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19. 42:] Written in year 14, third month of the pr.t-season, day 16 (= 16 Phamenoth). The one who speaks to Totoês, son of Imuthês, the Overseer of the Mystery of Osorbuchis: "perform burial for Hatrês, son of Lamenthis", is the scribe Pelaias, son of Pamenôs the elder, the priest who enters, of the first phyle. [Translation from Short Texts 2. 426, with corrections:] Written in year 14, 3rd month of the šmw season (Epeiph), day 6. The one who says before Tutu, son of Imhotep, <the> overseer of the mystery of Osorbuchis, 'Perform buial for Hatre, son of Lamenth(is),' is scribe Pelaiha, son of Pamenh the elder, the priest who enters, of the first phyle.

title: Schooltablet with alphabetic acrostic on Prometheus' creation of mortals
summary: The tablet contains a mythological story: an elaborated version of the creation of mortals out of clay by Prometheus.
translation_text: (Translation from P. L. Bat. 25) Modelling, Prometheus made mortals from the divine image (?). According to the wish of the gods - - - (he made?) mortals by hand (?). So, having mixed earth and water, he made - - - . Hard to tear away and dark, he - - - finished the hair. - - - putting eyebrows in front. As a bridge in the middle of the eyes he stretched out and made the nose. - - - twice - - - and to the temples he fastened. He made within the mouth the treasury of the tongue for the speech. Adequately he formed cheeks, he combined - - - with cheeks (?)

title: Schoolbook of Paulus: writing exercise (?) and loan of money
summary: Only a few traces of writing are visible on side I A. Side I B: content unclear.                                                   Side II A preserves the text of a loan of hundred myriads of denarii. The writing on II B has almost completely disappeared.

title: Schooltablet
summary: Content unclear

title: Schoolbook of Aurelius Antonius: Isocrates, Ad Demonicum 1; Word Division and Mathematics
summary: Schoolbook with a writing exercise; one page with word division and the table of 40

title: Cloth from the mummy of Didymos ~ Hatres
summary: Mummy cloth of Didymos
date_text: second half 2nd century CE: 141/2, 164/5 or 196/7 CE, 20 March
translation_text: [Translation from Short Texts 2. 1035, with correction:] (Greek text) Didymos the younger, son of Apuleius. He is sealed with (images of) hippocentaurodolphins. Year 5, Phamenoth (III pr.t) 24. (Demotic text) Hatre the younger.

title: Cloth from the mummy of Tatriphis
summary: Mummy cloth of Tatriphis
date_text: second half 2nd century CE: 142/3, 165/6 or 197/8 CE, 9 July
translation_text: [Translation from Short Texts 2. 1036] (Greek) 1. [Ta]atriphis, daughter of Kolanthos, 2. the son of Lykos. 3. The sixth year, Epeiph 15. (Demotic) 4. Ta<t>riphy, the daughter of Kola<n>jo, the son of L[ykos], 5. the son of Tartary (?), - - -.

title: Cloth from the mummy of Hierax
summary: Mummy cloth of Hierax
date_text: Second half 2nd century CE: 140/1, 163/4, or 195/6 CE, 1 August
translation_text: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 19. 29:] (Greek text) The fourth year, the 8th Mesore. Hierax, son of Psonsneou, weaver. (Demotic text) Hierax son of Psonsneus, weaver. The fourth year, the fourth month of šmw (i.e. Mesore), the eighth day. [Translation from Short Texts 2. 1034:] (Greek) 1. Fourth year, Me- 2. sore, eighth. 3. Hierax, 4. son of Pson- 5. sneu, 6. weaver. (Demotic) 7. Hierax, the son of (P)sonsneu, 8. weaver, 9. in year 4, 4th month of the šmw season (Mesore), day 8.
